_meta.description: Centralized authority registry for the immigration-guide plugin. For any T2/T3/T4 issue bundle, look up the mandatory source set here before concluding.
_meta.usage: Stage 3 of the 10-stage pipeline must consult this registry to identify mandatory sources before fetching. Stage 10 self-review validates the authority_set against mandatory sources.

tps_ead.label: TPS EAD automatic extension (all countries)

mandatory_sources.name: USCIS country-specific TPS page
mandatory_sources.controls: Designation period, eligible categories, designated-through date
mandatory_sources.when_mandatory: Any country-specific TPS question

mandatory_sources.name: Federal Register — country-specific TPS notice (most recent)
mandatory_sources.controls: Extension through dates, eligible category codes, re-registration windows
mandatory_sources.when_mandatory: Any extension date or re-registration question

mandatory_sources.name: USCIS I-9 Central
mandatory_sources.controls: Which categories qualify for I-9 automatic extension acceptance
mandatory_sources.when_mandatory: Any I-9 proof or employment authorization question

tps_ead.notes: Country is a parameter, not a separate bundle. Always check the country-specific FRN notice. Multiple extension paths may exist. Non-stacking rule may apply. Use tps-ead-resolver.py for deterministic branch selection after extracting dates.

ead_general.label: EAD / work permit validity and renewal (non-TPS bases)

mandatory_sources.name: USCIS Form I-765 page
mandatory_sources.controls: Filing instructions, eligible categories, category codes
mandatory_sources.when_mandatory: Any I-765 filing or EAD question

mandatory_sources.name: USCIS — Automatic EAD Extension (8 CFR 274a.13(d))
mandatory_sources.controls: 180-day auto-extension rule, which categories qualify, combo card rules
mandatory_sources.when_mandatory: Any EAD renewal or auto-extension question (non-TPS)

mandatory_sources.name: USCIS Policy Manual Vol 10 — Employment Authorization
mandatory_sources.controls: Detailed eligibility rules by category, adjudication standards
mandatory_sources.when_mandatory: Any detailed EAD eligibility or category question

ead_general.notes: Covers H-4 EAD, L-2 EAD, AOS-pending (c)(9), asylum (c)(8), DACA, OPT/STEM OPT, and all other non-TPS categories. Always identify the specific category code.

visa_bulletin.label: Visa bulletin priority dates and cutoffs

mandatory_sources.name: State Department Visa Bulletin (current month)
mandatory_sources.controls: Current priority dates for all family and employment preference categories
mandatory_sources.when_mandatory: Any priority date or visa availability question

mandatory_sources.name: USCIS — Which chart to use (Final Action vs Dates for Filing)
mandatory_sources.controls: Whether USCIS accepts Final Action or Dates for Filing chart this month
mandatory_sources.when_mandatory: Any I-485 filing window or chart-selection question

visa_bulletin.notes: Always get the current month's bulletin. Never answer priority date questions from training knowledge. Distinguish Final Action Date from Dates for Filing.

current_fees.label: USCIS filing fees

mandatory_sources.name: USCIS Filing Fees page
mandatory_sources.controls: Current fees for all USCIS forms
mandatory_sources.when_mandatory: Any fee question

mandatory_sources.name: Federal Register — USCIS Fee Schedule Rule
mandatory_sources.controls: Fee amounts by form type, fee rule effective dates, fee exemptions
mandatory_sources.when_mandatory: Any question about whether fees have recently changed or will change

mandatory_sources.name: USCIS Form I-912 (Fee Waiver)
mandatory_sources.controls: Fee waiver eligibility
mandatory_sources.when_mandatory: Always mention alongside fee amounts

current_fees.notes: Always mention Form I-912 fee waiver when discussing fees.

processing_times.label: USCIS processing times

mandatory_sources.name: USCIS Processing Times tool
mandatory_sources.controls: Current processing times by form type and service center
mandatory_sources.when_mandatory: Any processing time question

mandatory_sources.name: USCIS Case Status Online
mandatory_sources.controls: Individual case status, receipt number lookup, case processing stage
mandatory_sources.when_mandatory: When the user has a specific receipt number or wants their individual case status

mandatory_sources.name: USCIS Service Centers
mandatory_sources.controls: Which service center handles which form types
mandatory_sources.when_mandatory: When user doesn't know their service center

processing_times.notes: Processing times vary by service center. Always ask which service center.

h1b_cap_lottery.label: H-1B cap, lottery, and extensions

mandatory_sources.name: USCIS H-1B page
mandatory_sources.controls: Current cap, lottery rules, registration period
mandatory_sources.when_mandatory: Any H-1B cap or lottery question

mandatory_sources.name: USCIS Policy Manual Vol 2 — Nonimmigrant Workers
mandatory_sources.controls: Eligibility, specialty occupation, cap-exempt, extensions beyond 6 years
mandatory_sources.when_mandatory: Any H-1B eligibility, cap-exempt, or extension question

mandatory_sources.name: Federal Register — H-1B rules (most recent)
mandatory_sources.controls: Registration fee, lottery mechanics, registration period dates
mandatory_sources.when_mandatory: Any H-1B lottery rule or fee question

h1b_cap_lottery.notes: Cap-exempt employers (nonprofits, universities, gov research) bypass lottery. H-1B extensions beyond 6 years require I-140 or labor cert milestones.

advance_parole_travel.label: Advance parole and travel while AOS pending

mandatory_sources.name: USCIS Form I-131 (Advance Parole)
mandatory_sources.controls: AP rules, combo card availability
mandatory_sources.when_mandatory: Any travel-while-pending question

mandatory_sources.name: USCIS Policy Manual Vol 7 — AOS, Travel
mandatory_sources.controls: Effect of travel on pending I-485, abandonment, parole reinstatement
mandatory_sources.when_mandatory: Any question about travel abandoning I-485 or affecting parole

advance_parole_travel.notes: Travel without AP while I-485 pending can abandon the application. T3/T4 — always flag the risk.

marriage_aos.label: Marriage-based adjustment of status

mandatory_sources.name: USCIS Policy Manual Vol 7 — Adjustment of Status
mandatory_sources.controls: AOS eligibility, bars, entry requirements, §245(a) vs §245(i)
mandatory_sources.when_mandatory: Any AOS eligibility or bar question

mandatory_sources.name: USCIS Form I-485 page
mandatory_sources.controls: Filing instructions, concurrent filing, evidence requirements
mandatory_sources.when_mandatory: Any I-485 filing question

mandatory_sources.name: USCIS Form I-130 page
mandatory_sources.controls: Petitioner eligibility, relationship evidence, processing
mandatory_sources.when_mandatory: Any I-130 petition question

marriage_aos.notes: Entry method (inspected vs EWI) is critical. §245(i) grandfathering and parole-in-place are key branches. Always check for bars to adjustment.

naturalization.label: Naturalization / N-400 / citizenship

mandatory_sources.name: USCIS Policy Manual Vol 12 — Citizenship & Naturalization
mandatory_sources.controls: Continuous residence, physical presence, good moral character, English/civics
mandatory_sources.when_mandatory: Any naturalization eligibility question

mandatory_sources.name: USCIS Form N-400 page
mandatory_sources.controls: Filing instructions, early filing window, evidence requirements
mandatory_sources.when_mandatory: Any N-400 filing question

naturalization.notes: 3-year rule (married to USC) vs 5-year rule. Absences affect continuous residence: <6mo OK, 6-12mo rebuttable presumption, >12mo breaks it. Criminal history triggers GMC analysis — recommend attorney.

asylum_filing.label: Asylum filing and 1-year deadline

mandatory_sources.name: USCIS Asylum page
mandatory_sources.controls: One-year deadline, extraordinary/changed circumstances exceptions
mandatory_sources.when_mandatory: Any one-year deadline or asylum filing question

mandatory_sources.name: USCIS Policy Manual — Asylum
mandatory_sources.controls: Exception standards, eligibility bars, credible fear
mandatory_sources.when_mandatory: Any exception analysis or eligibility question

asylum_filing.notes: One-year deadline is outcome-determinative — T4 territory. Always flag attorney recommendation. Withholding of removal is the backup if deadline missed.

removal_defense.label: Removal proceedings and defense

mandatory_sources.name: EOIR Immigration Court Practice Manual
mandatory_sources.controls: Court procedures, filing deadlines, motions, continuances
mandatory_sources.when_mandatory: Any immigration court procedure question

mandatory_sources.name: USCIS Policy Manual — Removal grounds
mandatory_sources.controls: Grounds of inadmissibility, deportability, waivers
mandatory_sources.when_mandatory: Any removal ground or waiver question

removal_defense.notes: Always T4 — elevated attorney warning mandatory. Covers NTA, voluntary departure, cancellation of removal, prosecutorial discretion.

detention_bond.label: Immigration detention and bond

mandatory_sources.name: EOIR — Bond and custody procedures
mandatory_sources.controls: Bond hearing procedures, mandatory detention categories, redetermination
mandatory_sources.when_mandatory: Any bond or custody question

mandatory_sources.name: ICE Detention Standards
mandatory_sources.controls: Facility standards, access to counsel, medical care
mandatory_sources.when_mandatory: Any detention conditions question

detention_bond.notes: Always T4. Mandatory detention (aggravated felony, arriving aliens) vs discretionary. Bond amount, conditions of release, parole from detention.
